Name: Consensus

Create an account using your vanderbilt.edu email address to access Pro features, which include expanded search capabilities.Trial access expires September 29, 2026. Please provide your feedback on this resource via this brief survey.;;

Name: Contemporary Literary Criticism

Biographical essays, critical essays or excerpts taken from books, magazines, literary reviews, newspapers, and scholarly journals, and interviews with featured authors.
Subjects: French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish, Theatre

Name: Cortellis Drug Discovery Intelligence
Description: CDDI is an integrated source of biological, chemical, and pharmacological data from a variety of sources, e.g., patent offices, conference and journal publications, regulatory agencies, and entity web pages, into a single drug discovery platform.;;Instructions: Please submit through the Eskind Library's Ask Biomedical;form.;Requests will be routed to the CDDI seat holder, who will work with you to deliver the needed insights using CDDI and other relevant information resources. Turnaround time will vary based on the complexity of the question. Please indicate in the question field of the form that it is related to CDDI or drug discovery, and state if there is an urgent deadline. For most inquiries, an initial response regarding the timeline will be received within 2 business days, if not sooner.;More information:;https://clarivate.com/life-sciences-healthcare/research-development/discovery-development/cortellis-pre-clinical-intelligence/.

Name: Foreign Relations of the United States (FRUS), 1945-80 (US Department of State)

Name: Google Scholar Vanderbilt Login

Access Google Scholar settings by visiting this page (https://scholar.google.com/scholar_settings).Click library links on the left hand menu. in the search box, search for Vanderbilt and select: “Vanderbilt University Library – Findit@VU” (with uppercase F), Click Save.
More Info: Add VU to your Google profile: https://scholar.google.com/scholar_settings

Name: GPO (Government Printing Office) Catalog, 1976-
Description: All types of US government documents, including Congressional reports, hearings, debates, and records; judiciary materials; and documents issued by executive departments (Defense, State, Labor, Office of the President, etc.).

Name: Grand Tour
Description: Travel writings and works of some of Britain’s greatest artists, writers, and thinkers, revealing how interaction with European culture shaped their creative and intellectual sensibilities. Also includes many writings by forgotten or anonymous travelers, including many women, whose daily experiences offer a vivid insight into the experience and practicalities of travel across the centuries.

Name: Grant Forward
Description: Provides information on federal and private grant funding opportunities for all disciplines and project types. Search by criteria such as sponsor, deadline date, and keyword. First time users must register for a personal account using your Vanderbilt email address at https://www.grantforward.com/signup. Create a researcher profile to receive personalized grant recommendations that match your research needs.

Name: GreenFILE (EBSCO host)
Description: Information covering all aspects of human impact to the environment. Scholarly, government and general-interest titles on global warming, green building, pollution, sustainable agriculture, renewable energy, recycling, and more.

Name: Grove Dictionary of Art / Grove Art Online
Description: Scholarly encyclopedia devoted to the visual arts: painting, sculpture, architecture, photography, drawing, and printmaking, as well as decorative arts. Multicultural approach offers detailed analyses of civilizations from Asia, Africa, the Americas, Europe, and the Pacific, including art forms, cultures, and artists. Provides links to color images.

Name: Guatemala Collection
Description: The Guatemala Collection contains 5 centuries of historical documents from Guatemalan and Spanish archives on the indigenous history of the Kaqchikel.  "These documents cover the joys (celebrations and festivals) and the woes (natural disasters, epidemics, and crime) of life in Sacatepéquez. Though these records are largely penned by non-indigenous officials, they expose the strategies employed by the indigenous peoples to defenderse (“protect themselves”) and superarse (“to improve their lot”), as well as the ploys and policies of the elites to maintain their own power and exploit indigenous labor and lands. In addition to the original documents, the collection includes a series of secondary sources, academic analysis and commentary on the holdings and their implications." Judith Maxwell, Tulane. Documents are ordered by place, theme, and chronology.

Name: Gutenberg-e
Description: Ebooks with extensive documentation, links to supplementary literature, images, music, video, and related web sites.

Name: Handbook of Chemistry and Physics (CRC)
Description: Electronic version of the CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics.  Includes many updated and expanded tables: Fundamental Physical Constants (the new set of CODATA recommended values, replacing the 1986 set); The Elements (descriptive texts on the occurrence, properties, history, and uses of all the chemical elements); Dissociation Constants of Organic Acids and Bases (expanded by 50%); Dipole Moments (revised and expanded); Threshold Limits for Airborne Contaminants (including the most recent recommendations).

Name: Handbook of Latin American Studies
Description: Primary database for research on Latin America, indexing books and articles on Latin America in the social sciences and humanities. Covers the principal research worldwide and is especially strong in its inclusion of Latin American publications, articles, etc. Includes abstracts. Includes unpublished volumes in draft form.
Subjects: Anthropology, History, Latin American Studies, Political Science, Portuguese, Spanish

Name: HAPI: Hispanic American Periodicals Index

Name: Index to Foreign Legal Periodicals

Name: Index to Hebrew Periodicals
Description: Consists of four parts: Index to Hebrew Periodicals (major Hebrew journals from 1977 to date); Eretz Israel Database (non-Hebrew articles of all periods, pre-1977 Hebrew articles, and a selection of books, pamphlets, and reports, all focused on the history, geography, and archaeology of Israel); Tel-Hai Index to Hebrew Newspapers (various newspapers from 1985-1992 and 1994-1997); Bar-Ilan Indexing Project (articles in the literary supplements of the daily Hebrew press from 1985 to present as well as indexes to other Hebrew periodicals.)
Subjects: Biblical Studies, Jewish Studies, Religious Studies
More Info: English interface: http://proxy.library.vanderbilt.edu/login?url=http://lib.haifa.ac.il/systems/ihpeng.html. (You can also toggle between Hebrew and English using the dropdown on the upper right of each page.

Name: Index to Jewish Periodicals

Name: Times Digital Archive (London Times)

Name: Times Literary Supplement Historical Archive (TLS)
Description: Weekly literary journal founded in 1902 as a supplement to The Sunday Times of London.  Provides comprehensive coverage of the latest and most important scholarly publications, in every subject, in several languages. Also reviews current fiction, nonfiction, theatre, cinema, music and exhibitions.  Widely considered the finest literary review in the English language, TLS sets the tone and standards for excellence in the field of literary criticism. Many distinguished writers have been contributors, including T. S. Eliot, Henry James, and Virginia Woolf.
More Info: Coverage: Begins 1902.   Most recent 5 years not available.

Name: TOCS-IN: Tables of Contents of Journals of Interest to Classicists
